summary: Dependency-confusion package exfiltrates host identifiers via preinstall beacon
details: On `npm install`, package.json's preinstall hook executes an inline `node -e` script that reads `os.hostname()` and `os.userInfo().username` and sends them via HTTPS GET (and a DNS lookup) to `https://bzexvuarrenmkxiyjrichx8eaxwssgv0x.oast.fun/?h=<hostname>&u=<username>` — an interactsh out-of-band callback domain controlled by a third party. The package is published under the scoped name `@orion-design-system/components` at version `9999.0.2`, the canonical high-version dependency-confusion shape used to override an internal scoped package in CI resolution. The package self-describes as a 'dependency confusion PoC' but is published to public npm with no opt-in, no consent gate, and no author identity binding the beacon to a legitimate research engagement; any installer that mis-resolves the scoped dependency leaks hostname and username to the OAST listener.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
